name: openmark-router
description: Benchmark-driven model routing plugin for OpenClaw, powered by OpenMark AI (https://openmark.ai). Registers as a provider, performs one isolated low-cost classification call, and lets the routed model produce the real reply on the same turn. The package includes a bundled Python routing engine and uses temporary OpenClaw runtime, config, and session-state updates to keep same-turn routing aligned with benchmark data and compatibility fallback behavior. The router captures your real default model for classifier/passthrough use, auto-detects what providers your OpenClaw setup can use, prefers direct provider keys first, and falls back to OpenRouter keys when benchmark rows include them and OpenRouter is available. It is still useful for single-provider, subscription-backed, OAuth-backed, and free-model-heavy setups because you can benchmark and route within the access you already have. In workloads that are overusing flagship models, benchmark-driven routing can often reduce spend substantially, and some workloads may see savings on the order of 50-80% or more depending on task mix and benchmark results. Includes a local dashboard for router health, detected providers, benchmark visibility, benchmark CSV import and deletion, category descriptions, and basic controls such as routing strategy and routing-card display. Validated on OpenClaw CLI and Telegram; other surfaces may also work but are not yet exhaustively validated. Provider filtering is best-effort and still depends on valid OpenClaw model ids. Prefer small cheap models for classification when you want the classifier cost to be negligible. Provider billing, quotas, and rate-limit behavior remain provider-dependent. Restart the OpenClaw gateway after install, update, or changing your real default model.
author: openmarkai
